sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Paper Cut Layered — combining hand-crafted, layered, dimensional elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value hand-crafted and layered design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Paper Cut Layered style use case illustration
Paper Cut Layered style use case illustration
Paper Cut Layered style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Paper Cut Layered — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: light (#fef8f0). The .inner panel has a clean background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #e88d3c as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #2a1a0a for headings, #7a6a5a for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of paper cut layered through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Quicksand — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #e88d3c. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #7a6a5a. Buttons: primary — #e88d3c background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #e88d3c border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), sans-serif stack.
